hidden_gems.name: Abel Mendoza Monge
hidden_gems.address: Carretera de Penacerrada 7, 26338 San Vicente de la Sonsierra, La Rioja, Spain
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Abel Mendoza and his wife Maite work 37 small plots over roughly 18 hectares on the left bank of the Ebro around San Vicente, Abalos and Labastida. They bottle single-variety whites from Viura, Malvasia, Garnacha Blanca and Tempranillo Blanco and treat Graciano as seriously as Tempranillo, making precise, silky wines admired by sommeliers far more than by tourists.
hidden_gems.tip: The white grape varietal bottlings are the connoisseur's pick; allocations are small and move through specialist merchants rather than the cellar door.
hidden_gems.description: A 37-plot grower-producer in San Vicente de la Sonsierra making precise single-variety whites and serious Graciano. A reference for left-bank Sonsierra terroir.

hidden_gems.name: Bodega Tentenublo Wines
hidden_gems.address: Camino del Soto 22, 01307 Vinaspre, Alava, Spain
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Roberto Olivan farms around 10 hectares of old vines near the hamlet of Vinaspre in the foothills of the Sierra de Cantabria, working field blends with spontaneous fermentation, minimal sulfites and a light hand with oak. The wines are electric, aromatic and divisive in the best way.
hidden_gems.tip: Look for the village-and-vineyard bottlings rather than the entry wine; the Escondido and single-site cuvees show what old-vine Vinaspre can do.
hidden_gems.description: Roberto Olivan's low-intervention, old-vine project near Vinaspre, making vivid field-blend Rioja with spontaneous ferments and minimal sulfites.

hidden_gems.name: Bodegas Bhilar
hidden_gems.owner: David Sampedro Gil and Melanie Hickman
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: David Sampedro farms 17 hectares of estate vineyards in Elvillar that are certified organic and biodynamic, working the soils with plough-horses and an off-grid solar cellar built in 2016. The Phincas single-plot wines are some of Rioja Alavesa's most thoughtful expressions of place.
hidden_gems.tip: The estate was certified biodynamic by Demeter in 2021; the Phincas range is the one to taste for the single-vineyard story.
hidden_gems.description: A Demeter-certified biodynamic estate at Elvillar in Rioja Alavesa, ploughed by horse, making site-driven Phincas wines from organic old vines.

hidden_gems.name: Olivier Riviere Vinos
hidden_gems.address: Calle Breton de los Herreros 16, 26500 Calahorra, La Rioja, Spain
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: A Cognac-born, Saint-Emilion-trained winemaker who came to Spain with Telmo Rodriguez, Olivier Riviere farms around 25 hectares of owned and leased plots organically with biodynamic practices, building a Burgundy-style hierarchy of village and single-site Riojas from old, high vines.
hidden_gems.tip: His outlook is terroir-first rather than oak-tier; the village and lieu-dit cuvees reward seeking out over the entry wines.
hidden_gems.description: A French-born grower making terroir-led, organically farmed Rioja across Rioja Oriental and Alta, with a Burgundy-inspired village-and-site hierarchy.

hidden_gems.name: Bodegas Honorio Rubio
hidden_gems.address: Calle Mayor 12, 26323 Cordovin, La Rioja, Spain
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: A family cellar in Cordovin, up the Najerilla valley, championing the near-lost clarete and macerated white styles. Fermentations start spontaneously and sulfite use is held back until fining, giving honest, low-intervention wines that flag a vanishing Rioja tradition.
hidden_gems.tip: Seek out the macerado white and the clarete; they are a window into Rioja styles that the big houses largely abandoned.
hidden_gems.description: A Cordovin family winery reviving Rioja clarete and skin-contact whites with spontaneous fermentation and minimal added sulfites.

hidden_gems.name: Pedro Balda
hidden_gems.address: 26338 San Vicente de la Sonsierra, La Rioja, Spain
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: A pioneer of natural-leaning Rioja from small plots in San Vicente de la Sonsierra, working without pesticides or systemic treatments, hand-harvesting only the ripest fruit and bottling unfiltered with no added sulfites. Balda is candid that he is neither certified organic nor biodynamic, just relentlessly natural.
hidden_gems.tip: Majuelo de la Nava is the wine to find; production is tiny and these go to natural-wine specialists, not supermarkets.
hidden_gems.description: A pioneering natural-wine grower in San Vicente de la Sonsierra making unfiltered, no-added-sulfite Rioja from old plots.

hidden_gems.name: Bodegas Lacus
hidden_gems.address: Calle Cervantes 18, 26559 Aldeanueva de Ebro, La Rioja, Spain
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Luis Arnedo, fifth-generation grower in Aldeanueva de Ebro, farms 36 hectares organically in warm Rioja Oriental, using no chemical fertilisers or herbicides and only oak when it genuinely helps. The wines are fresh, fine and refreshingly free of heavy extraction.
hidden_gems.tip: Rioja Oriental Garnacha is the calling card here; the wines show the warmer, fruitier side of the region with restraint.
hidden_gems.description: A small organic family cellar in Rioja Oriental making fresh, low-oak Garnacha and Tempranillo from Aldeanueva de Ebro.

hidden_gems.name: Vinedos de Paganos
hidden_gems.address: Carretera de Navaridas s/n, 01309 Paganos, Laguardia, Alava, Spain
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Founded by Marcos and Miguel Eguren in 1998, this Paganos estate built rock-cut underground cellars to age single-vineyard Tempranillo from El Puntido and La Nieta. The vines are worked biodynamically, and the wines are among Rioja Alavesa's most precise and mineral expressions.
hidden_gems.tip: La Nieta is the cult bottle; El Puntido is the more available single-vineyard introduction to the estate's style.
hidden_gems.description: An Eguren-family single-vineyard estate at Paganos in Rioja Alavesa, ageing mineral Tempranillo from El Puntido and La Nieta in rock-cut cellars.
